vocabulary knowledge passive vocabulary
what is passive vocabulary and do we
have active vocabulary part two passive
versus active vocabulary passive
vocabulary is like this if you see the
word somewhere or if you hear it or if
you watch it or if you read it anywhere
you may remember the meaning but when
you are trying to speak or WR you cannot
use it this is passive so you may know
the meaning back in your head but you
cannot use it however active vocabulary
on the other hand is the words that you
have learned before and you frequently
use them to communicate that means you
frequently use them in speaking or
writing now which one is better of
course you would try to make your
passive vocabulary active and this is
the key if you want to be able to use
the words that you have learned when you
are communicating you need to make them
active now here's an important thing to
know you learn new words they're added
to the passive vocabulary knowledge and
then if you don't practice what will
happen you will forget you will forget
the words and then again you will use
text or audio or video learn new words
add them to your passive knowledge and
then again don't practice and forget and
then again you go around and around and
around in circles you will get caught in
this vicious cycle of learning new words
and then forgetting them and learning
and forgetting and learning and
forgetting and this is really
frustrating for students you may feel
like I'm doing something wrong or that
the language is very difficult or that
I'm not smart enough but none of these
is true you are just not trying to make
the past the word active but how will
you do that there are three things you
should do part three how to activate in
order to make your passive vocabulary
active you need to do three things
WP write practice review start with
write it goes without saying that when
you learn new words you need to write
them down otherwise how are you going to
remember what the words were but how
will you write them down this is very
tricky and very important for instance
let's say I want to read a text and from
this text I'm going to learn some new
words look at the text within this text
I have identified some words that I want
to learn exercise Health Fitness
strength cardio Wellness relationships
friendship support communication trust
quality time loyalty affection
connection nutrition love understanding
empathy now it's time to write down
these words and something that I have
seen a lot of students do is this
exercise relationships wellness support
cardio affection Love empathy endurance
strength but what is wrong with this
method these are all random words
written together right I mean yes they
come from the same text and from the
same context but there is no clear link
between the words for example what's the
link between exercise and relationship
or between empathy and strength however
another way that you can take notes and
write down the words is this I have
identified two categories health and
fitness family friendship and
relationships under health and fitness I
will write exercise strength endurance
cardio and wellness under family friends
and relationships I would write loyalty
communication trust affection and
empathy what I've done is so simple I've
just categorized them right under
certain topics now what you can see is a
group of words that have a strong link
there is a common theme there is a
Common Thread that connects all these
words together this is called learning
words in a topic based approach so you
select a topic and under that topic you
list the words related in this way in
your brain you can easily categorize
them and next time you're talking about
health and fitness you will remember all
the words that you've written it will
help you remember them more easily so
step one one was writing step two is
practice how will you practice at the
end of each word list at the end of each
topic vocabulary list that you've
written it is a good idea to write a
small sentence or a small paragraph
trying to use as many of those words and
phrases as you can for example at the
end of my list under health and fitness
I'm going to write a sentence like this
regular exercise is key to improving
strength endurance and cardio Health
leading to overall Wellness as you can
see it's just a sentence but in this
sentence I've used 1 2 3 four five of
the new words similarly under family
friends and relationships I'm going to
write something like this loyalty is
built on open communication trust and
real affection and that's how we develop
empathy again in this one sentence I
have used loyalty communication trust
affection and empathy five of the new
words that I have written down now what
does this help you with first you you
practice using the words that you have
learned in written form that means
you're basically pushing the words to
come out of the passive box in your head
and go towards the active box and
secondly this will help you in the
review stage which we're going to talk
about now how will you review from time
to time go back to your notebook and
then instead of reading the words and
their meanings read the sentences that
you have written or the short paragraphs
that you have written at the end of it
now how is this helpful first you will
see the words in context you're not
seeing the words alone but in a text
second the text is written by you so
it's very familiar for you and third
because they all relate to the same

talking about that topic bam they're
there so the overall diagram is like

your passive vocabulary knowledge if you
do not practice you will forget but if
you follow the wpr steps you will
activate your vocabulary and eventually
your activated vocabulary will be used
in spoken or written English and that's
it next time you're learning new words
